New Man Computer beats Perplexity Computer. AI just broke out of the cloud and it took over your entire computer. Not your browser, not a tab, your whole machine. This is brand new. This is big. And honestly, it is a little scary. Let me show you exactly what just happened. So, right now there is a war going on. A war between AI companies and they are all fighting over one thing. Who gets to control your computer first? Perplexity dropped their computer agent. Open AAI has been working on operator but now Manis just did something that nobody saw coming. They went local and that changes everything. Let me break it down for you piece by piece because once you see what this thing can do, you are not going to look at your desktop the same way again. So here's what happened. Manis just released a new feature called my computer and it runs inside their desktop app. Not in a browser, not in the cloud, right there on your actual machine, your PC, your Mac, whatever you are using. And this AI agent can now read your files, edit your files, open your apps, run terminal commands, and use your own hardware to do it. Your GPU, your processor, all of it. Think about that for a second. Before this AI was stuck in a little sandbox. It could answer questions. It could write stuff, but it could not actually do anything on your computer. Now it can. And that is a massive shift. Hey, if we have not met already, I am the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of Goldie Agency. Whilst he is helping clients get more leads and customers, I am here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Okay, so let me tell you why this matters. Think about your desktop right now. You have got folders everywhere, files you forgot about, apps you barely use, and every single day you are doing the same boring tasks over and over, moving files, renaming stuff, running the same commands. What if an AI agent could just do all of that for you? That is what madness my computer does. You give it a task. It asks for your approval and then it goes and does it on your actual machine, not some cloud server, your computer. Let me give you some real examples. Say you've got thousands of files on your desktop, just a mess. You tell man to organize them. It reads every file. It figures out what they are. It renames them. It puts them in folders. It tags them. Done in minutes. Something that would take you hours or maybe even days. Gone. Just like that. And here is where it gets really wild. They showed a demo where Manis built an entire Mac app from scratch using the terminal in about 20 minutes. Think about that. A full working app built by an AI on your local machine. No cloud needed, no complicated setup. It just did it. That is insane. And it does not stop there because Manners can use your GPU. So if you have got a decent graphics card sitting there doing nothing most of the day manners can put it to work running machine learning models processing data doing heavy compute tasks. Your computer becomes smarter just by having this thing installed. And here is the part that blew my mind. You can send tasks from your phone. You are out at lunch. You think of something you need done. You send it from your phone. Manus picks it up and runs it on your PC back at home or at the office. Remote control through AI. That is next level. Now, I know what some of you are thinking. Is this safe? And that is a fair question because giving an AI full access to your computer sounds terrifying. But here is how Manis handles it. Every single command needs your approval before it runs. You are the boss. Manis is the worker. It suggests what it wants to do. You say yes or no. Simple. So, it is not just going rogue and deleting your files or something. You stay in control. But yeah, I am not going to lie. It is still a little bit wild to think about. Now, let me talk about something you have probably been wondering. How does this compare to Plexity computer? Because Perplexity dropped their computer use agent and people went crazy over it. And look, Plexity is great. I use it. But here is the key difference. Perplexity works through your browser. It can control websites. It can click buttons. It can fill in forms. But it is stuck in the browser. It cannot touch your files. It cannot open your apps. It cannot run terminal commands. It does not have access to your operating system. Manus does. Malice is not controlling a website. It is controlling your entire computer. That is a completely different level. Okay, so let me keep going because there is more. Let me lay out the comparison clearly. Manus gives you full operating system access. Perplexity gives you browser access. Manus has full command line control. Plexity has limited actions inside web pages. Manus uses your local GPU and hardware. Plexity runs on cloud servers. Manners can run background tasks while you do other stuff. Perplexity needs the browser open. So when you put them side by side, it is not even close in terms of what you can actually do on your machine. Plexity controls websites. Manus controls your entire computer. That is the simplest way to think about it. Now here is where I want to get real with you for a second because this is not just about one tool. This is about a trend, a big one. AI started in the cloud, then it moved into your browser. Now it is moving into your operating system. And this is just the beginning. Every single computer is going to have an AI agent built into it soon. Think about it like this. Right now, you open apps and you do tasks inside them. In the future, you are going to tell an AI what you want done and it is going to open the apps for you, run the tasks for you and give you the results. You become the manager. AI becomes the worker and that flips everything we know about using computers. Now let me talk about some use cases that I think are going to be huge. First one is development. If you are a developer, you work with developers. Having an AI that can run terminal commands and build stuff locally is a gamecher. No more copying and pasting code between chat GPT and your editor. The AI just builds it right there. Second is file management. If you run a business and you've got thousands of documents and files and spreadsheets, Manis can organize all of that for you automatically. Third is background automation. You can set up workflows that run on your machine even when you are not there. That is like having a virtual assistant that never sleeps and never takes a break. Fourth is using old hardware. Got an old laptop collecting dust? Install Manus on it and turn it into an AI worker that runs tasks for you in the background. That is brilliant. But here is the thing I want you to remember. We are still early. This is version one of local AI agents. It is going to get better. It is going to get faster. It is going to get smarter and the people who start learning this stuff now are going to be so far ahead of everyone else it is not even funny. So do not sleep on this and look there are risks. I am not going to pretend there are not. Giving AI access to your file system is a big deal. Security matters. You need to be careful about what you let it do. Always review the commands before you approve them. Do not just click yes on everything. Be smart about it. But the potential here is massive. And I think the approval system Manis has built in is a solid first step. So, let me wrap this up. Manage my computer is a big deal. It moves AI from the cloud to your desktop. It gives AI real access to your files, your apps, your terminal, and your hardware. It lets you automate tasks that were impossible to automate before. And it does it all with an approval system so you stay in control. Is it perfect? No. Is it the future? I think so. And I think you need to pay attention to this.
